title: GL.iNet VPN Routers: Flint 2, Beryl AX, and Slate 7 Compared for VPN Use
description: GL.iNet makes some of the most VPN-friendly routers available. This guide covers the Flint 2, Beryl AX, Beryl 7, and Slate 7, with real WireGuard throughput numbers, OpenWrt access, and which model makes sense for home vs. travel use.
content:
## Why GL.iNet Routers Are Different

Most consumer routers treat VPN as an afterthought. GL.iNet builds around it. Every router in their lineup ships with OpenWrt-based firmware that has a VPN client baked directly into the admin interface. You do not need to flash anything, hunt for `.ovpn` files, or SSH in to get WireGuard running. You paste a config or enter credentials and it works in under five minutes.

The product line covers two use cases: travel routers (small, battery-friendly, bus-luggage-sized) and home routers (faster, better range, fixed placement). The right choice depends on what you are doing.

## The Models Worth Knowing

### Beryl AX (GL-MT3000) — Best Wi-Fi 6 Travel Router

The Beryl AX is a compact dual-band Wi-Fi 6 router with a MediaTek MT7981B processor running at 1.3 GHz. It draws under 8 W, which means it can run off a decent USB-C power bank for hours. That makes it genuinely portable.

WireGuard throughput on the Beryl AX reaches around 350-400 Mbps in practice. OpenVPN lands around 90-120 Mbps. For most hotel or Airbnb setups, the Beryl AX is more than enough.

It runs OpenWrt 21.02-based firmware. You can install packages via `opkg` from SSH, which gives you AdGuard Home, Tailscale, and most standard OpenWrt networking tools.

### Slate 7 (GL-BE3600) — Wi-Fi 7 Travel Router

The Slate 7 is the Beryl AX's successor with Wi-Fi 7 support. WireGuard throughput hits around 490 Mbps, and OpenVPN-DCO (Data Channel Offload) reaches 385 Mbps. These are measured on LAN, not over a real WAN connection, but they show the headroom is there.

The Slate 7 runs OpenWrt 23.05, which is a more current base than the Beryl AX. The `opkg` package repository is larger and more up-to-date. AdGuard Home comes pre-installed.

One limitation: the Slate 7 is dual-band and does not include a 6 GHz radio despite being Wi-Fi 7. If you specifically need 6 GHz, look elsewhere.

### Beryl 7 (GL-MT3600BE) — Fastest Travel Router

The Beryl 7 is GL.iNet's newest travel router and the fastest one they have made. WireGuard throughput reaches 1,100 Mbps. It supports USB-C PD charging, which means a single cable from a laptop charger or power bank powers it cleanly.

If you regularly work from locations with fast internet and run everything through a VPN, the Beryl 7 eliminates the speed cap that affects older GL.iNet travel routers.

### Flint 2 (GL-MT6000) — Best Home Router

The Flint 2 is GL.iNet's home router, with a MediaTek Filogic 830 chip, 1 GB RAM, and two 2.5GbE ports. WireGuard throughput is around 900 Mbps. That covers most gigabit fiber connections without a bottleneck.

It supports VLANs, SQM (cake/fq_codel), AdGuard Home, and Tailscale out of the box. You can run the entire home network through NordVPN or Mullvad while keeping IoT devices on a separate VLAN without VPN.

### Flint 3 (GL-BE9300) — Wi-Fi 7 Home Router

The Flint 3 brings Wi-Fi 7 and tri-band (2.4/5/6 GHz) to the Flint line. However, early reviews note that WireGuard performance on the Flint 3 is actually slower than on the Flint 2, due to how the newer chipset handles kernel-level WireGuard. This is expected to improve with firmware updates, but as of mid-2026 the Flint 2 remains the better VPN router between the two.

## Setting Up a VPN Client

1. Log into the admin panel at `192.168.8.1`.
2. Go to **VPN > VPN Dashboard > VPN Client**.
3. Select your protocol: WireGuard or OpenVPN.
4. For WireGuard: paste the config from your VPN provider, or use the provider-specific setup wizard if your provider is listed (NordVPN, ProtonVPN, Mullvad, ExpressVPN, and others are built in).
5. Toggle the connection on.

GL.iNet's firmware supports multiple simultaneous VPN client connections with policy routing. You can send specific IP ranges or devices through different tunnels, or exclude certain traffic from the VPN entirely.

## OpenWrt Access

All GL.iNet routers expose the full OpenWrt interface at `192.168.8.1/cgi-bin/luci`. You can also SSH in with `ssh root@192.168.8.1`. From there, the standard OpenWrt tools work: `opkg update && opkg install <package>`.

For users who want to run custom WireGuard configs, nftables rules, or install packages not in the GL.iNet app store, the underlying OpenWrt layer is fully accessible without any additional flashing.

## Travel Router Use Case: Connecting Via Hotel Wi-Fi

The main travel scenario: connect the GL.iNet router to a hotel Wi-Fi network as a repeater client, then connect your devices to the GL.iNet's own Wi-Fi. All traffic routes through the router's VPN tunnel.

1. Go to **Internet > Repeater** and scan for the hotel network.
2. Connect using the hotel credentials.
3. Make sure VPN is on before connecting your devices.

This setup protects all devices including game consoles and smart TVs that have no native VPN app.

## Which VPN Works Best on GL.iNet

**NordVPN** has a dedicated wizard in the GL.iNet admin panel. You authenticate with your NordVPN token, pick a server or region, and the router generates and applies the WireGuard config automatically. Key rotation is handled by the firmware. This is the easiest setup of any VPN on these routers, and NordVPN's server count (7,000+ servers across 118 countries) means you will always find a fast nearby server. For most users buying a GL.iNet router specifically for VPN use, [NordVPN](https://nordvpn.com) is the obvious pairing.

**Mullvad** also works well, particularly if you want port forwarding or multi-hop. Mullvad configs can be generated from their web config generator and imported as standard WireGuard files.

**ProtonVPN** works via manual WireGuard config import. Their configs include DNS settings that prevent leaks, which is worth keeping.

## Summary: Which Model to Buy

- **Travel, under $80:** Beryl AX (GL-MT3000)
- **Travel, want Wi-Fi 7:** Slate 7 (GL-BE3600) or Beryl 7 (GL-MT3600BE)
- **Home, gigabit VPN:** Flint 2 (GL-MT6000)
- **Home, want Wi-Fi 7:** Wait for Flint 3 firmware to mature, or buy the Flint 2 now
